input.background: Refer to the previous exercise. The mean survival time in the IDX-treated group was 116 days (SE1=5.6). The mean survival time in the control group was 88.5 (SE2=1.9).
input.question: Test H0: μ1=μ2.

output.answer:
A. Hypotheses. H0: μ1=μ2 versus Ha: μ1 ≠ μ2
B. Test statistic. SE=√(SE1^2+SE2^2)=√(5.6^2+1.9^2)=5.91 and t_stat=(116-88.5)/5.91=-4.65. Since n1=10 and n2=10, df_conserv=10-1=9.
C. P-value. The one-sided P-value is between 0.0005 and 0.001 (via Appendix Table C). Therefore, the two-sided P-value is between 0.001 and 0.002. Using www.cytel.com/Products/StaTable/, the two-sided P-value=0.0012. The evidence against the null hypothesis is highly significant.
D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against the null hypothesis is significant at α=0.01.

mean_survival_IDX.description: Mean survival time in the IDX-treated group.

SE_IDX.description: Standard error of the mean survival time in the IDX-treated group.

mean_survival_control.description: Mean survival time in the control group.

SE_control.description: Standard error of the mean survival time in the control group.

H0.description: Null hypothesis stating that the mean survival times in the two groups are equal.
